title: AI Thinks? Really? Show Me.

modules.title: Robots, Rules, and Real Brains
modules.sub: Discover what makes AI different from a human brain
modules.description: Learners dive into a mystery adventure and meet their quirky AI sidekick, immediately confronting the question of whether AI truly thinks. Through interactive comparisons, they explore how robots and AI follow rules rather than feelings or instincts, setting the stage for everything that follows.

modules.title: Patterns Everywhere: How AI Sees
modules.sub: Train your eyes to spot the patterns AI relies on
modules.description: Learners examine everyday objects and scenarios to uncover the hidden patterns that AI systems detect and use. By sorting clues in the mystery adventure, they practise recognising repeating shapes, colours, and sequences the same way an AI model does when it processes information.

modules.title: AI Versus Human: Who Decides?
modules.sub: Compare human judgment with AI rule-based decision making
modules.description: Learners face a series of mystery-solving dilemmas where they choose an answer and then watch how their AI sidekick reaches the same — or a different — conclusion using logic and rules. This side-by-side comparison helps them articulate what AI can handle well and where human thinking still has the edge.

modules.title: Build Your Own Decision Tree
modules.sub: Construct a real decision tree that solves a problem
modules.description: Learners design and build their own decision tree to crack the final mystery puzzle, mapping out yes-or-no questions just like a real AI would. By the end they have a working, hand-crafted decision tree they can explain to others, connecting everything they learned about patterns, rules, and AI thinking.

synopsis: Through a choose-your-own mystery adventure, you and a quirky AI sidekick solve puzzles that reveal how artificial intelligence actually processes information — no magic involved. By the end you will have built a simple decision tree that makes choices the same way a real AI does.
rationale: Without a concrete, accurate model of what AI is, young learners default to science-fiction extremes — either fearing AI as all-powerful or dismissing it as a simple toy — neither of which prepares them for responsible use.
learning_outcome: Students can distinguish AI from human thinking, explain what a pattern and a decision tree are, and construct a working decision tree that solves a simple real-world problem.
source_signals: What Is AI? | Learn all about artificial intelligence
